one_line_thesis: Post-Solventum 3M trades at ~$155 with thin owner cash (~$2.58/sh) versus ~$6/sh dividends; base return -12.2% per year → watch until PFAS cash cadence clarifies.

deep_dive.executive_summary:
3M is a post-Solventum industrial franchise with improving segment operating income but depressed owner cash after PFAS litigation payments and portfolio restructuring. FY2025 revenue was $24.9 billion (+1.5%); operating income $4.6 billion; net income $3.3 billion; cash from operations $2.3 billion; capital spending $0.9 billion; free cash flow about $1.4 billion ($2.58 per share). Q1 2026 revenue $6.0 billion (+1.3% year over year) with operating income $1.4 billion (+12%).

At $155.34 per share, we expect about -12.2% per year over seven years on filing-based owner cash of $2.58 per shar...

decision.primary_power_zone: Capital cycle and normalized industry economics
decision.next_action: Freeze the evidence packet and select three method-independent raters.

valuation.interpretation: value_per_share is withheld while any material additive component is unpriced; priced_components_per_share is not a complete security value.

scenario_contract.rule: Cases must change cited causal operating, capital, probability, timing, or financing drivers—not only a terminal multiple.

change_control.rule: A source fact is locked. Every assumption change requires a reason, author, timestamp, and before/after value.

optionality.rule: Every option requires a beneficiary, probability, timing, remaining capital, failure case, and non-overlap control; absence of option value must be explicit rather than unvalued.

committee.next_action: Freeze the evidence packet and select three method-independent raters.

method_fit.label: Capital cycle and normalized industry economics
applicability_tests: Industry capacity, additions, closures, and replacement costs can be measured.
Current margins can be located relative to a cycle rather than extrapolated.
Maintenance and growth capital are separated.
Leverage and liquidity survive the low case.
failure_modes: replacement cost used for assets that cannot earn their cost of capital

validation_cohort.purpose: Test depletion, commodity exposure, finite reserves, and royalty-unit reconciliation.
Test tangible capital, credit losses, funding, regulation, and excess-capital realization.
Test owner earnings, capital-light reinvestment, cyclicality, and buyback value creation.
Test normalized margins, industry supply response, replacement cost, and reinvestment timing.
Test product concentration, pipeline probabilities, patent cliffs, and catalyst timing.

method_fit.rule: Use one primary method and no more than two corroborating methods; irrelevant methods and personas remain silent.

outcomes.weighting_rule: Do not change a persona's relevance within a power zone until at least 20 attributable, dividend-aware outcomes exist there; never reweight automatically.

attribution.explanation: A complete security value is intentionally withheld while material components are unpriced. Attribution restarts after the first proof-complete baseline.

total_return_panel.error: Vendor events are discovery evidence; primary-source completeness has not been established.
total_return_panel.return_contract: split-adjusted close excluding dividends; event amounts normalized for later splits; cash reinvested at ex-date close; pre-tax nominal

coverage.note: Vendor events are discovery evidence; primary-source completeness has not been established.

valuation_decision.primary_power_zone: Capital cycle and normalized industry economics
valuation_decision.next_action: Freeze the evidence packet and select three method-independent raters.

personas.falsifier: FCF yield < 0.5% or commoditized archetype (turnaround/optionality only)
personas.label: Chris Hohn / TCI

personas.falsifier: dhando -> none OR net-debt/EBITDA > 3 OR bear return < -15%
personas.label: Mohnish Pabrai

personas.falsifier: No asset intensity / pricing power (moat eroding + non-croupier)
personas.label: Murray Stahl
personas.why_silent: Universe criteria not met: hard_asset_archetype, moat_durable

personas.falsifier: FCF yield below universe median OR base IRR below median
personas.label: Joel Greenblatt
personas.why_silent: Universe criteria not met: earnings_yield_high, quality_proxy

personas.falsifier: Operating lens only — no dated inefficiency / catalyst
personas.label: John Mihaljevic / MOI

personas.falsifier: No dated contractual payoff or royalty/trust structure
personas.label: Horizon Kinetics

active_lenses.label: Horizon Kinetics
active_lenses.falsifier: No dated contractual payoff or royalty/trust structure

timeline.label: Net income moved -22.1% versus the prior period (4,173.0 to 3,250.0).
Basic EPS moved -20.2% versus the prior period (7.6 to 6.0).
Q1 2026 earnings: revenue +1.3% YoY, operating income +12% YoY; full-year 2026 guidance reiterated
FY2025 10-K filed: $24.9B revenue, $4.6B operating income, $2.3B operating cash flow; three-segment structure post-Health Care separation
FY2025 repurchased ~$3.3B of common stock while paying $3.3B dividends; share count down to ~530M
Solventum (Health Care) spin-off completed; 3M retained three segments: Safety and Industrial, Transportation and Electronics, Consumer
Public water suppliers PFAS class-action settlement framework; ongoing litigation accruals and cash payments
Announced Solventum spin-off of Health Care business and commitment to exit PFAS manufacturing by end of 2025
Committed to exit PFAS manufacturing and discontinue PFAS use across product portfolio by end of 2025
Mike Roman CEO era: portfolio simplification, litigation management, and spin execution

industry.structure: Global diversified industrials with scale R&D, manufacturing, and distribution. Post-Solventum, 3M competes in safety/industrial abrasives and PPE, electronics materials, and consumer brands. Concentrated share in niche SKUs but broad competition in each category.
industry.share_shift: 3M regaining operating momentum in Safety and Industrial (+3.9% revenue, +14% operating income FY2025) while Transportation and Electronics revenue declined slightly; Consumer flat. PFAS exit removes a liability overhang but also former product lines.
industry.trend: Portfolio simplification (Health Care spin, PFAS exit), litigation settlement cadence, and industrial end-market normalization. Buybacks and dividends remain large relative to reported free cash flow.
